accrued - (of a benefit or sum of money) received or accumulated in regular or increasing amounts over time.
adamant - refusing to be persuaded or to change one's mind.
adjourn - postpone (a meeting) with the intention to continue it later
anointed - to have holy oil or water applied to you in a religious ceremony, or more commonly in modern use, to be formally chosen or singled out for an important role, position, or honor.

aphorism - an aphorism (from greek ἀφορισμός: aphorismos, denoting 'delimitation', 'distinction', and 'definition') is a concise, terse, laconic, or memorable expression of a general truth or principle.[1] aphorisms are often handed down by tradition from generation to generation.
awry - away from the usual or expected course; amiss. out of the normal or correct position; askew.

behest - is an authoritative order, command, or strong, urgent prompting. in modern english, it is most commonly used in the formal phrase "at the behest of", which means you did something because someone else asked or ordered you to do it.
bleak - (of the weather) cold and miserable.

caveat - a caveat is a warning. when someone adds a caveat to something they're telling you to beware — maybe what they're telling you comes with certain conditions or maybe there's something dangerous lurking.

pervasive - pervasive is an adjective that describes something spreading widely throughout an area or a group of people.

subsidiary - a company that is owned by a larger company
